stimulus: Some pollinator-network models predict that hummingbirds will reduce their daily travel distances as nectar-bearing flowers become more numerous, but ornithologists Pia Castillo and Lin-Wei Huang showed that hummingbird behavior departs from this prediction whenever flowers in dense patches are ______: the birds will travel beyond locally abundant but uniform patches in order to obtain a mix of corolla shapes and nectar concentrations.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The colon explains that the birds travel beyond dense patches "to obtain a mix of corolla shapes and nectar concentrations." That motivation only makes sense if the dense patches lack variety—i.e., are "homogeneous."

Choice B is incorrect because depleted patches no longer offer nectar at all, so seeking variety would not be the reason the birds leave. Choice C is incorrect because the sentence specifies that the patches in question are dense, contradicting the idea of dispersal. Choice D is incorrect because immaturity describes a developmental stage rather than the lack of varietal mix the colon highlights.
